metadata.objective: Build comprehensive analytics and A/B testing system to track, measure, and continuously optimize cold outreach campaigns for all 3 businesses

stories.title: Build campaign performance dashboard
stories.description: Create analytics dashboard that shows key metrics per business: response rates, conversion funnel, template performance, time-series trends. Supports filtering by business_id, date range, template, pain point.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.campaign_analytics dashboard --business marceau-solutions
Shows: total sent, delivery rate, response rate, hot/warm/cold breakdown
Template leaderboard: top 3 templates by response rate
Funnel visualization: Contacted → Responded → Qualified → Converted
Time-series: response rates over last 7/30/90 days
Comparison view: marceau-solutions vs swflorida-hvac vs shipping-logistics
Export to CSV/JSON for further analysis

dashboard_metrics.response_rate: % leads that replied (target: marceau 12-14%, hvac 8-10%, shipping 6-8%)
dashboard_metrics.time_to_response: Hours from send to first reply (median)

stories.title: Implement A/B testing framework
stories.description: Build A/B testing system to compare template variants. Split leads into control/variant groups, track performance separately, calculate statistical significance, declare winner.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.ab_testing create --name 'pain_vs_social_proof' --control no_website_intro --variant social_proof_intro --sample-size 100
Automatically splits next 100 leads: 50 control, 50 variant
Tracks responses separately per group
python -m src.ab_testing results --name 'pain_vs_social_proof' shows: control response rate, variant response rate, statistical significance, winner (if significant)
85% confidence threshold for declaring winner
Minimum 100 contacts per variant before declaring
Winning template auto-promoted to primary, loser archived

stories.title: Build multi-touch attribution system
stories.description: Track which touch in the 7-touch sequence drives responses. Attribution: did they reply on Day 0 (intro), Day 2 (followup #1), Day 5, etc? Optimize sequence based on data.
acceptance_criteria: Track response_touch_number (0-6) when lead replies
python -m src.campaign_analytics attribution --business marceau-solutions
Shows: % responses per touch (e.g., 10% respond on touch 0, 30% on touch 2, 25% on touch 3...)
Identifies most effective follow-up messages
Recommends: 'Touch 3 drives 30% of responses - prioritize this message'
Drop-off analysis: where do we lose leads in the sequence?
stories.notes: CHECKPOINT: Review dashboard, A/B testing, attribution before optimization algorithms

stories.title: Create template performance scoring system
stories.description: Score templates on multiple dimensions: response rate, qualification rate (hot/warm %), conversion rate, opt-out rate. Composite score ranks templates, identifies winners/losers.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.campaign_analytics template-score --template no_website_intro
Scores on 5 dimensions: response rate (40% weight), qualification rate (30%), conversion rate (20%), opt-out rate (5%), delivery rate (5%)
Composite score 0-100
python -m src.campaign_analytics templates --business marceau-solutions --sort-by score
Leaderboard: top templates by composite score
Recommendation: 'Use no_website_competitor_v1 (score: 87) for new campaigns'
Archive templates scoring <50 after 100+ sends

stories.title: Build cohort analysis system
stories.description: Group leads into cohorts (by scrape date, business category, pain point, geographic area) and compare performance. Identify: which cohorts respond best? Which segments to prioritize?
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.campaign_analytics cohorts --group-by category
Shows: gyms 12% response, salons 10%, restaurants 8%
python -m src.campaign_analytics cohorts --group-by pain_point
Shows: no_website 14%, few_reviews 8%, no_online_transactions 6%
python -m src.campaign_analytics cohorts --group-by scrape_date
Identifies: 'Week of Jan 6-12 had 15% response (best), Week of Jan 13-19 had 7%'
Recommendations: 'Prioritize gyms + no_website (highest response combo)'

cohort_dimensions.geographic: Naples vs Fort Myers vs Bonita Springs
cohort_dimensions.business_size: Small (<10 reviews) vs Medium (10-50) vs Large (50+)

stories.title: Implement predictive lead scoring
stories.description: ML-based lead scoring: predict likelihood of response based on business attributes (category, review count, website presence, etc). Prioritize high-probability leads.
acceptance_criteria: Train simple logistic regression: features = [category, review_count, has_website, location], target = did_respond
python -m src.lead_scoring score --input scraped_leads.json
Outputs: lead_score (0-100) for each lead
python -m src.lead_scoring prioritize --business marceau-solutions --limit 100
Returns top 100 leads by predicted response probability
Validation: top 20% scored leads should have 2x response rate vs bottom 20%
Re-train model weekly with new response data
stories.notes: CHECKPOINT: Review all analytics systems before automation/recommendations

stories.title: Build automated optimization recommendations
stories.description: AI-powered recommendation engine that analyzes all campaign data and suggests: which templates to use, which cohorts to target, when to send, what to A/B test next.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.campaign_optimizer recommend --business marceau-solutions
Analyzes: template scores, cohort performance, A/B test results, attribution data
Recommendations: 'Use no_website_competitor_v1 for gyms (18% response). A/B test social proof vs scarcity angle for salons. Pause few_reviews campaign (4% response, below target).'
Auto-generates A/B test ideas: 'Test: Pain point message vs Social proof for restaurants'
Schedule optimization: 'Tue 11 AM has 2x response rate vs Thu 2 PM - adjust timing'
Budget allocation: 'Allocate 60% budget to gyms (highest ROI), 30% salons, 10% restaurants'

recommendation_types.template: Which template to use for next campaign

stories.title: Create weekly optimization report automation
stories.description: Automated weekly email report to William with: campaign performance, A/B test results, cohort insights, optimization recommendations. Auto-send every Monday 8 AM.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.weekly_report generate --send
Email includes: Overall performance (all 3 businesses), Top performing templates, A/B test winners, Cohort insights, Optimization recommendations, Action items for the week
Formatted as HTML email with charts/tables
Cron job: Weekly on Monday 8 AM
Example action item: 'A/B test winner: social_proof_intro beat pain_intro by 30% (p<0.05). Recommend switching primary template.'
Links to detailed dashboards for deep-dive

report_sections.action_items: Specific tasks (change template, adjust timing, etc)

statistical_significance.early_stopping: Can declare winner at 85% confidence, regardless of sample size if difference is large

ml_model.training_data: All historical campaigns with response labels
ml_model.retraining: Weekly, as new data comes in

optimization_frequency.daily: Check A/B tests for significance, update template scores
optimization_frequency.weekly: Generate recommendations, send report, retrain ML model

success_metrics.ab_test_velocity: 2-3 active tests per business at all times
